title: RunHandle
description: Output of `pilot.tools.submit run`. Captures everything needed to (a) re-derive the launched training job, (b) poll its status, (c) cancel it, and (d) replay the exact env diff Pilot applied. Persisted at `state/runs/<run_id>/handle.yaml`. Read by `submit cancel`, `submit status`, and downstream `observe.snapshot`.

schema_version.description: RunHandle schema version. Bumped on breaking changes.

run_id.description: Stable identifier for this submission. Used as the directory name under `state/runs/<run_id>/` and the cache key for observe.snapshot.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when submit dispatched the subprocess (not when training started).

cluster_config_ref.description: Absolute path to the cluster.yaml that was used for this submission. Recorded so downstream tools (cancel/status/observe) can replay the same launch contract.

plan.description: What was launched, both as user-supplied baseline and as the effective version after Pilot applied any overrides.

base_ref.description: Absolute path to the user-supplied Primus exp.yaml passed via `--plan`.

effective_ref.description: Absolute path to the post-override yaml that was actually fed to Primus via `EXP=...`. Lives at `state/runs/<run_id>/plan.effective.yaml`.

overrides.description: Map of `<key> -> <value>` patches Pilot applied on top of `base_ref`. Keys target the `modules.pre_trainer.overrides` block of the Primus yaml (so `train_iters` -> `modules.pre_trainer.overrides.train_iters`).

launch.description: Concrete launch parameters derived from cluster.yaml and the LaunchPlan.

cmd.description: Shell command actually executed (single-mode: `bash <PRIMUS>/examples/run_pretrain.sh`; slurm-mode: `srun --jobid=<jid> ... bash run_pretrain.sh`).

env_diff.description: Env vars Pilot explicitly set on top of inherited environment. Includes EXP, NNODES, GPUS_PER_NODE, NODE_RANK, MASTER_ADDR, MASTER_PORT, TRAIN_LOG. Recorded for replayability.

pid.description: PID of the parent shell process (single-mode only). Used by `submit cancel` to send SIGTERM.

slurm_step_id.description: SLURM step id (e.g. `12345.0`) returned by srun, used by `scancel` to terminate just this submission without releasing the parent allocation.

stdout.description: Absolute path to the captured stdout/stderr stream (combined).

stderr.description: Optional separate stderr file. Null when stderr is folded into stdout.

exit_code_sentinel.description: Path to the file the wrapper writes the exit code to once the subprocess terminates. Polled by `submit status` to distinguish `running` from `completed`/`failed`.

status.description: Initial status when handle is written. Stays `launching` for the first liveness window (~5s); flips to `running` once subprocess survived liveness; later updates by `submit status` reflect process state.

exit_code.description: Filled by `submit status` (or the foreground path) once the subprocess terminates. Null while `status` is `launching`/`running`/`unknown`.

wallclock_s.description: Wallclock seconds between created_at and termination. Filled when status moves to a terminal state.
